issues_classified.description: CRITICAL FORMULA-DATA INCONSISTENCY: The paper defines Ea = -ln(c0) where c0 is consistency fraction at k=16, but the actual code computes Ea = 1/c0 where c0 is a fitted saturation parameter from curve_fit(bounds=[0.1, 10.0]). For c0=0.10565 (fitted), 1/c0=9.465 matches data; -ln(0.10565)=2.247 does not. The notation.md Usage Note 3 incorrectly states 'Ea ~9.47 corresponds to c0 ~0.106' under Ea = -ln(c0), which is mathematically impossible. The paper's c0 (consistency fraction) and code's c0 (fitted parameter) are different variables with the same name.
issues_classified.suggestion: Resolve the naming collision and formula mismatch: (1) In analysis_h3.py and analysis_h2.py, rename the fitted parameter from 'c0' to 'tau' or 'c_sat' to avoid confusion with consistency fraction; (2) Update the paper's Section 3.2 to state the actual formula used: Ea = 1/tau where tau is the characteristic consistency convergence scale from fitting C_k = c_inf * (1 - exp(-k/tau)); (3) Update notation.md to reflect the correct formula and variable definitions; (4) Verify all reported Ea values are consistent with the corrected formula. Estimated effort: 30 minutes. Verification: re-run analysis_h3.py and check that output Ea values match paper claims.

issues_classified.description: H3 SOURCE DATA MISLABELED AS 'CONFIRMED': analysis_h3.json h3_evaluation.status='CONFIRMED' with note 'Ea is a useful routing signal', but predictor_quality.auc=0.436 and spearman_ea_accuracy=-0.063 (p=0.66) show zero predictive power. The evaluation logic only checks threshold pass (low_ea_accuracy >= 0.75) and ignores AUC/Spearman.
issues_classified.suggestion: Fix the H3 evaluation logic in analysis_h3.py: require BOTH threshold pass AND auc > 0.5 for CONFIRMED status. Update analysis_h3.json and analysis_h3_summary.md to reflect FALSIFIED status with explanation: 'H3 is FALSIFIED because AUC=0.436 < 0.5 and Spearman=-0.063 (p=0.66) show zero predictive power. The 75% threshold pass is achieved via post-hoc optimization and does not constitute genuine predictive signal.' Estimated effort: 15 minutes. Verification: re-run analysis_h3.py and confirm output status is FALSIFIED.

issues_classified.description: MISSING BIBLIOGRAPHY: Paper cites [1] through [11] but has no References section. Citations cannot be verified. 'Li 2026' and 'ACAR 2026' appear to be future-dated and may not exist as citable works.
issues_classified.suggestion: Add a complete References section to paper.md with full bibliographic entries for all 11 citations. For each citation: verify existence (search arXiv for IDs 2508.16456, 2601.00828, 2602.21231), include author names, title, venue/year, and URL/arXiv ID. If 'Li 2026' or 'ACAR 2026' cannot be verified, replace with verified references or remove the citation. Estimated effort: 20 minutes. Verification: every [N] citation in the paper has a corresponding entry in References.

issues_classified.description: TABLE 8 (ERROR CLASSIFICATION) LACKS QUANTITATIVE DATA: Section 6.2 claims 'Execution errors dominate low-Ea failures' but Table 8 only has qualitative descriptions and examples. No counts, proportions, or statistics support this central diagnostic claim.
issues_classified.suggestion: Either (a) perform manual quantitative error classification on all low-Ea failures (~12-15 problems with Ea below threshold), categorize each as execution/correctness/extraction error, add Count and Proportion columns to Table 8, or (b) soften the claim from 'Execution errors dominate' to 'We hypothesize that execution errors dominate based on qualitative inspection' and note quantitative classification as future work. Estimated effort for (a): 30 minutes; for (b): 5 minutes. Verification: Table 8 either contains numeric counts or softened claim language.

issues_classified.description: POST-HOC THRESHOLD = DATA LEAKAGE: The 'optimal threshold' of 9.9999999999 is optimized on the same evaluation data. The 75% figure and 25pp 'irreducible error floor' derived from it are optimistic upper bounds, not measured quantities.
issues_classified.suggestion: Clarify in Section 6.3 that the 25pp gap is an optimistic upper bound from post-hoc threshold optimization, not a measured floor. Add language: 'This 25pp gap represents the maximum theoretical improvement achievable with perfect knowledge of the optimal threshold; any real-world system must estimate the threshold from training data and will perform worse due to threshold estimation error, distribution shift, and other confounds.' Consider computing a cross-validated threshold estimate for a realistic lower bound. Estimated effort: 10 minutes. Verification: Section 6.3 explicitly labels 25pp as an upper bound.

issues_classified.description: SAMPLE SIZE TOO SMALL FOR ROBUST CONCLUSIONS: n=50 problems is pilot-scale. Only 2 problems at Level 1, 13 at Level 5. No confidence intervals reported for key statistics (Spearman=0.448, AUC=0.436, R2=0.924).
issues_classified.suggestion: Add bootstrap confidence intervals for all key statistics in analysis scripts: (1) For R2: bootstrap resample problems 1000 times, fit exponential each time, report 95% CI; (2) For Spearman: bootstrap resample, compute correlation each time, report 95% CI; (3) For AUC: bootstrap resample, compute AUC each time, report 95% CI. Add these CIs to the paper's results sections. Acknowledge n=50 as a limitation prominently. Estimated effort: 45 minutes. Verification: analysis output files contain 'ci_95' fields and paper reports intervals.

issues_classified.description: PER-PROBLEM FIT FAILURE UNDERMINES H1 CLAIM: Median R2=0.000 across all models, 80% fit failure, only 8/50 prefer exponential by AICc. Aggregate R2=0.924 may be an averaging artifact, not evidence of a genuine physical law.
issues_classified.suggestion: Expand discussion in Section 5.1 and Section 6: (1) Add bootstrap CI for aggregate R2 to assess robustness; (2) Explicitly discuss that the exponential form may be the best group-level curve fit rather than a genuine mechanism; (3) Clarify '80% fail to fit' criterion (define as R2 < 0.1 or failure to converge). The 'critical caveat' in Section 5.1 is good but needs stronger language about the implication. Estimated effort: 20 minutes. Verification: paper contains explicit discussion of averaging artifact possibility.

issues_classified.description: NOVELTY CLAIM IS NARROW AND FRAGILE: Novelty rests entirely on H3 negative result. H1/H2 collide with Yang et al. (2025). ACAR (2026) reports conceptually related 'agreement-but-wrong' ceiling (~8pp). Paper does not clearly differentiate consistency-based vs variance-based routing.
issues_classified.suggestion: Strengthen differentiation from ACAR in Section 2 and Section 6: (1) Add explicit contrast table: consistency-based (our focus, Ea from answer agreement) vs variance-based (ACAR, sigma from answer distribution spread); (2) Quantify the larger error floor: ~25pp (consistency) vs ~8pp (variance); (3) Emphasize that our diagnostic analysis (error classification) goes beyond ACAR's finding. If ACAR cannot be verified as a real paper, remove the comparison and frame as standalone contribution. Estimated effort: 20 minutes. Verification: Section 2.4 or Section 6 contains explicit differentiation paragraph.

issues_classified.description: SINGLE MODEL LIMITS GENERALIZABILITY: All experiments use Qwen2.5-Math-7B-Instruct on 50 problems. Core claim about consistency-based routing is presented as general but evidence supports only this model.
issues_classified.suggestion: Temper generalizability claims throughout the paper: (1) Abstract: change 'consistency measures answer stability, not correctness' to 'on Qwen2.5-Math-7B-Instruct, consistency measures answer stability, not correctness'; (2) Introduction: add caveat that results are for one model and one dataset; (3) Limitations section: expand discussion of single-model limitation and call for replication. If time permits, run the same experiment on DeepSeek-Math-7B (baseline 26% was already measured in Round 2) to test generalizability. Estimated effort for tempering: 15 minutes; for second model: 4 hours. Verification: abstract and conclusion contain model-specific qualifiers.

issues_classified.description: H5 FALSIFICATION IS UNDER-REPORTED: H5 (consistency-Ea matches saturation-k0) is falsified (Spearman=-0.219, p=0.54, only 10/50 valid k0 pairs) but only briefly mentioned in Section 5.4. This challenges internal consistency of the theoretical framework.
issues_classified.suggestion: Expand H5 discussion in Section 6: add a new subsection (6.4) or expand existing discussion to explain why consistency-derived Ea and saturation-derived k0 diverge. Key points: Ea measures answer stability (agreement among samples), k0 measures convergence rate to asymptotic accuracy; they capture different constructs; 'difficulty' is not a unitary construct. Estimated effort: 15 minutes. Verification: Section 6 contains expanded H5 discussion with construct differentiation.

issues_classified.description: INCONSISTENT TERMINOLOGY: 'Arrhenius kinetics' vs 'Arrhenius-like kinetics' appears inconsistently. Glossary prefers 'Arrhenius-like kinetics' but Section 5.1 heading uses 'Aggregate Arrhenius Saturation'. Table 7 status capitalization inconsistent.
issues_classified.suggestion: Standardize terminology: (1) Replace all 'Arrhenius kinetics' with 'Arrhenius-like kinetics' or 'exponential saturation'; (2) Update Section 5.1 heading to 'Aggregate Arrhenius-Like Saturation'; (3) Standardize Table 7 status entries to consistent formatting (e.g., all lowercase with bold for 'falsified' only, or bold all status entries). Estimated effort: 10 minutes. Verification: grep paper.md for inconsistent terms returns zero matches.

issues_classified.description: SECTION 3.2 PRODUCT INTERPRETATION IS MISLEADING: 'The product P_infty * k0 has units of samples and can be interpreted as a problem-difficulty parameter' conflates ceiling and rate parameters.
issues_classified.suggestion: Remove or rephrase the sentence in Section 3.2: either delete entirely, or rephrase as 'For problems with similar P_infty, larger k0 indicates slower convergence toward the ceiling.' This avoids conflating ceiling and rate. Estimated effort: 5 minutes. Verification: sentence is removed or rephrased.

issues_classified.description: 'PROVE' IS TOO STRONG FOR STATISTICAL EVIDENCE: Section 5.3 states 'These values prove Ea cannot predict single-pass solveability.' Glossary recommends 'confirmed'/'falsified' over 'proven'/'disproven'.
issues_classified.suggestion: Replace 'prove' with 'demonstrate' or 'show' in Section 5.3 and search paper.md for any other instances of 'prove'/'proven'/'disproven'. Estimated effort: 5 minutes. Verification: grep for 'prove' in paper.md returns no matches in claims context.

issues_classified.description: MISSING APPENDIX CONTENT: Outline plans appendices (per-problem fits, Ea distribution, full problem data table, extraction audit) but none are present.
issues_classified.suggestion: Add minimum appendix content: (1) Table of all 50 problems with columns: idx, Level, Ea, c0 (fitted), final_consistency, single_pass_correct, k0 (if available); (2) Brief description of answer extraction methodology. This strengthens reproducibility. Estimated effort: 20 minutes. Verification: appendix section exists in paper.md with full problem data table.

issues_classified.description: LEVEL 5 Ea SHOWS ALGORITHMIC SATURATION: All 13 Level 5 problems have Ea ~10.0 with sigma ~1.9e-6, suggesting computational artifact rather than genuine consistency measurement.
issues_classified.suggestion: Investigate and explain the Level 5 artifact: (1) Report raw consistency values (c0 fitted parameter and final_consistency) for Level 5 problems in the paper; (2) Explain that all Level 5 problems have very low consistency (final_consistency ~0.33-0.67) and the fitted c0 hits the lower bound (0.1), causing Ea = 1/0.1 = 10.0 to cluster; (3) Note this as a limitation in Section 6.5. Estimated effort: 15 minutes. Verification: Section 6.5 contains explanation of Level 5 artifact.

issues_classified.description: H4/H5 PROMISED BUT NOT DELIVERED: alternatives.md proposes H4 (error analysis) and H5 (entropy routing) as next steps, but paper presents H4 as qualitative Table 8 without data and H5 as 'pending' in Table 9.
issues_classified.suggestion: Reframe the paper to match what was delivered: either (a) complete H4 quantitative analysis and add H5 entropy routing experiment (high effort), or (b) reframe as focused falsification study (H1-H3-H5 only) and move H4/H5 to Future Work section. Given resource constraints, option (b) is recommended. Estimated effort: 10 minutes. Verification: paper structure matches delivered content.

issues_classified.description: GPU IDLE TIME AND INEFFICIENT SCHEDULING: Multiple tasks failed due to dependencies (setup_api_environment, g0_baseline_eval, eval_g3_adaptive_routing) wasting GPU allocation time. g1_saturation failed after 200 minutes (answer extraction) then had to be re-run as g1_saturation_v2.
issues_classified.suggestion: Improve task planning to avoid dependency chains that block GPU utilization: (1) Check API key availability before scheduling API-dependent tasks; (2) Run pilot experiments (n=5-10) before full experiments to catch answer extraction issues early; (3) Parallelize independent tasks (g0_baseline and g1_saturation could run in parallel on different GPUs). The pilot (n=30) should have caught the extraction issue before the full run (n=50). Estimated effort: planning improvement for next iteration. Verification: next iteration's task plan includes pilot gates and parallelization.

issues_classified.description: STALE/FAILED TASKS ACCUMULATE IN EXPERIMENT STATE: experiment_state.json contains many stale tasks from prior rounds (train_g1_stepdpo, eval_g3_adaptive_routing, setup_api_environment) that are irrelevant to current paper, causing confusion during recovery.
issues_classified.suggestion: Clean up experiment_state.json by archiving old iteration tasks to exp/history/ before starting a new iteration. Add a cleanup step to the iteration initialization process. Estimated effort: 10 minutes. Verification: experiment_state.json contains only tasks relevant to current iteration.

success_patterns: Honest reporting of negative results: AUC=0.436 and per-problem fit failure (median R2=0.000) reported without spin across 6 consecutive iterations
Clear experimental protocol with well-structured group design (G0 baseline, G1 saturation, G2 consistency, G3 routing) and explicit hypotheses with thresholds
Data properly saved in structured JSON format enabling cross-validation of paper claims against raw data
Adaptive pivoting across rounds: EDW-DPO (Round 1) -> CCAR (Round 2) -> Activation Energy Theory (Round 4) based on evidence
Strong caveats and limitations section showing intellectual honesty (critical caveat in Section 5.1, limitations in Section 6.5)
PyTorch 2.11.0 successfully installed and validated for Blackwell sm_120, enabling future GPU training experiments
systemic_patterns: Formula/notation mismatch between paper text and experiment code: paper describes one computation but code implements another. This is a recurring pattern across iterations where theoretical framework and implementation drift apart.
Hypothesis evaluation logic checks only one metric (threshold pass) while ignoring others (AUC, Spearman), leading to contradictory labels between source data and paper narrative.
Small sample sizes (n=30-50) labeled as 'full' experiments when they are pilot-scale, leading to unstable statistics and wide confidence intervals.
Post-hoc optimization presented without adequate caveats, creating data leakage and optimistic performance bounds.
Promised analyses (H4 error classification, H5 entropy routing) not delivered, making the paper feel incomplete.

suggestions: Add pilot gate (n=5-10) before full experiments to catch extraction/formatting issues early, saving ~200 minutes of failed runs
Parallelize independent tasks: g0_baseline and g1_saturation can run simultaneously on different GPUs
Check API key availability before scheduling API-dependent tasks to avoid immediate failures
Archive stale experiment state between iterations to reduce recovery confusion
Analysis tasks (analysis_h1, analysis_h2, analysis_h3) are CPU-only and can run in parallel; no GPU needed

recommended_focus: Resolve the Ea formula-data inconsistency before any further writing or submission
Fix H3 evaluation logic to correctly report FALSIFIED status
Add complete bibliography with verified references
Add bootstrap confidence intervals for all key statistics
Temper generalizability claims to model-specific language
